frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: compression adapts to recent stimulus statistics to match resource allocation

frameworks.name: logarithmic encoding
frameworks.one_line: static log-transform of magnitude posited as intrinsic perceptual primitive

core_question: Does compressive number-to-space mapping reflect a static logarithmic transform or dynamic adaptation to recent stimulus statistics?
key_assumptions: number-line task indexes internal number-to-space mapping
recent stimulus history can reshape perceptual encoding
compression shape is a measurable signature of the underlying code
manipulations: stability vs variability of the stimulus distribution
adaptive number-line task parameters across 4 experiments
key_findings: magnitude and shape of compressive bias depend on recent stimulus distribution
stable distributions drive compression toward linearity
interpretations: number-to-space mapping is a context-adapting dynamic process
compression is not a fixed perceptual primitive nor a static logarithmic transform
